description: Confounder: The model assumes apply() with a lambda returning floats always yields float64,

_meta.explanation: The rule 'When a pandas Series of integer dtype has apply() called with a lambda that returns floats, the resulting Series automatically promotes to float64 dtype.' does NOT hold here. The model assumes apply() with a lambda returning floats always yields float64, but when even one element returns a non-numeric type (string), the result promotes to object dtype instead.
_meta.curriculum_note: Rule 'When a pandas Series of integer dtype has apply() called with a lambda that returns floats, the resulting Series automatically promotes to float64 dtype.' holds in the obvious case but breaks here. Distractor B is the hard negative — the rule naively applied.
